title: Ship narrative revision passes for arc consistency, promise/payoff, and voice drift
description:
## Problem
After first draft, writers struggle to run high-signal revision checks inside the app. Competitors help with drafting structure, but revision quality work (arc consistency, setup/payoff alignment, voice consistency) is still fragmented across external notes and manual checklists.

## Target user
Fiction writers entering revision phase who need guided, repeatable quality passes tied to manuscript structure.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of completed revision passes per active manuscript
- Reduce unresolved continuity/setup-payoff issues by final draft stage
- Increase manuscript completion rate after first-draft milestone

## Scope
- Add Revision Passes panel with three pass templates: Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift
- Each pass generates a checklist of scene-linked review items and statuses (open, accepted, dismissed)
- Allow writers to create custom notes per review item and mark final disposition
- Add pass summary view with unresolved items by chapter/scene
- Save revision history snapshots so writers can resume passes later

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting prose
- Grammar/spellcheck replacement
- General-purpose AI critique outside defined pass templates

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items
- Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note
- Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context
- Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later
- Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report

## Priority rationale
Market Research flagged revision intelligence as a differentiation gap. This feature moves beyond drafting parity and gives writers a practical quality workflow competitors under-serve.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

comment.body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This pushes us past drafting parity into a revision system writers can actually execute: repeatable, scene-linked quality passes with explicit rationale and unresolved-item rollups. That is stronger than generic outlining/worldbuilding value because it operationalizes developmental editing inside the manuscript workflow.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter positions itself around "plotting, organization, smart story elements" and a smoother writing flow, which indicates strong drafting/structure emphasis rather than explicit guided revision passes (source: https://livingwriter.com/ ; also mirrored in page metadata on the public site).
- Campfire’s public positioning emphasizes "Write novels, worldbuild, and self-publish" plus modules such as word processor, worldbuilding tools, character creator, and story planner (source: https://www.campfirewriting.com/features/).
- Our own baseline in `docs/INSPIRATION.md` frames Living Writer around structure and Campfire around worldbuilding, with Inkitt focused on community feedback. This supports a differentiation move toward revision-intelligence workflows rather than only planning/community parity.

## Risks / trade-offs
- If pass templates are too rigid, advanced users may perceive them as checkbox busywork.
- Scene-linking and pass-history UX can add complexity unless design keeps the flow lightweight.
- Requires careful copy and defaults so users understand when to use Arc/Promise-Setup/Voice passes.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but require Design to keep pass start/resume flow to two clicks from manuscript context and make unresolved items visible without overwhelming the editor.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
